text.title: Internationalization Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Internationalization application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: User-facing strings live only in catalogues; component code uses t(key) calls. Lint rule flags string literals in JSX or template files.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded strings cannot be translated; lint enforcement is the cheapest gate.

rule.statement: Plurals, gender, and quantity-dependent strings use ICU MessageFormat (`{count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}}`); concatenated strings are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Concatenation breaks for languages with non-English plural rules (Russian: 1, 2-4, 5+); ICU handles them all.

rule.statement: Numbers, dates, and currencies are formatted via Intl (browser) or CLDR (server). Manual formatting strings are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Manual formatting hardcodes assumptions (comma vs dot decimal separator); Intl reads the locale and does it right.

rule.statement: CSS uses logical properties: margin-inline-start, padding-block-end, text-align: start. Physical left/right is allowed only for direction-fixed elements (e.g. cursor, mathematical notation).
rule.rationale: RTL retrofits with left/right become a multi-day rewrite; logical properties make the swap free.

rule.statement: Pseudo-localization runs in CI: transforms catalogue values (Réàl Téxt becomes [Ŕéàĺ Ţéx̌ť]) and renders pages. Truncation and missing keys are flagged.
rule.rationale: Pseudo-loc surfaces hardcoded strings (they show up un-transformed) and tight layouts before any real translation.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
